_comment: Image generation prompts for gpt-image-2. Customize stylePreset to match brand aesthetic, then define 8-15 image entries. Run: npm run gen-images. See PLAYBOOK.md for known gpt-image-2 quirks (org verification gate, edit endpoint, size constraints, transient 500s).
_workflow: 1) Fill in stylePreset with 5-7 brand-specific sentences (colors, lens, mood). 2) Add image entries. 3) Place headshot JPG at scripts/headshot-source.jpg. 4) npm run gen-images. 5) Preview at /en/review (dev only). 6) Reroll failures with: npm run gen-images -- --only slug1,slug2 --force
stylePreset: TODO: 5-7 sentence brand-specific style preset. Example: 'Editorial photograph, magazine quality. [BRAND PALETTE] color palette — [COLOR_1 hex], [COLOR_2 hex], [COLOR_3 hex]. [LIGHT CHARACTER — e.g. soft natural light, golden hour]. Subtle film grain. [COMPOSITION STYLE — e.g. negative space dominant, rule of thirds]. Minimal — no people unless specified, no clutter, no text overlay. [LENS CHARACTER — e.g. 35mm or 50mm, Portra 400-style emulsion]. No HDR, no sharpening.'

images._comment: Hero section background — wide landscape format. Light, airy, no people.

images._comment: KNOWN ISSUE: gpt-image-2 edit endpoint is legacy-locked to dall-e-2 (returns 400). Workaround: use headshot-source.jpg directly in with CSS filter: grayscale(1). See PLAYBOOK.md. This entry is kept for reference but will fail at runtime.
images.prompt: convert to warm B&W editorial portrait, preserve facial detail, soft Portra 400 emulation, magazine cover quality
